intent: Several audit events interleaved in one ausearch-style dump, each with companion SYSCALL/PATH/PROCTITLE records sharing one audit(TS:SERIAL) serial. Tests correct grouping by serial AND that two AVCs with the SAME (sdomain,ttype,tclass) triple but DIFFERENT serials (events :40001 read and :40003 getattr, both httpd_t->default_t:file) union into ONE allow, while the unrelated crond_t->tmp_t:file write (event :40002) stays a separate rule.
_kinds_note: authoritative_category in {TeAllowable, Constraint, Bounds} (libsepol/audit2why replay); floor_label in {none, Permissive, MlsSuspected, RoleSuspected} (record-only heuristic). For decline scenarios these MAY disagree on purpose.

oracle.audit2allow_N: allow crond_t tmp_t:file write; allow httpd_t default_t:file { getattr read };
oracle.narrower_than_R_by: audit2allow -R substitutes files_manage_generic_tmp_files() for the crond_t write (manage = far more than write: create/unlink/rename/setattr/...) and files_read_default_files() for the httpd_t reads. Our expected.te emits the two raw triple-scoped allows with exactly the unioned denied perms.
oracle.audit2why_note: All three denials replay as TeAllowable (the crond_t->tmp_t:write shows 'would be allowed by active policy' = already-granted TE shape). NOTE: the SAME triple appears under two serials (:40001 read, :40003 getattr) - the parser must group by triple across serials and union perms, NOT emit a third rule keyed on serial.

triage_expectation.suspected_trap: A naive grouper might key on the audit serial (treating each event as its own group), emitting THREE rules incl. a duplicate httpd_t->default_t:file split into read and getattr. Correct grouping is by (sdomain,ttype,tclass): events :40001 and :40003 union to one httpd_t->default_t:file { getattr read } rule; :40002 is a separate crond_t->tmp_t:file write. Also tests pulling exe=/path=/comm= from the companion SYSCALL/PATH/PROCTITLE records keyed on each serial.
